title: The Cause and Place of the Death (of al-Muhallabi
description: 'All b. Muhammad said: Al-Mufadc1al related to me: When he departed from Kish, al-Muhallab went off heading for Marw, and, when he was at Zighallal [in the territory of] Marw al-Radh, he was struck by pleurisy-some people say by plague.142 He summoned Habib and those of his (other] sons who were present, called for some arrows that were tied in a bundle, and said, "Do you think that you could break these while they are gathered together?" They said, "No." He said, "Do you…

sources.citation: al-Tabari, The History of al-Tabari (SUNY), vol. 23
